title: Botryopteris crenata
taxonomy: Botryopteris crenata is classified as a fern, more specifically as a member of the family Dipteridaceae. It is within the genus Botryopteris, which is a group of extinct ferns. The species is known to be part of the Botryopteris genus, which consists of fossil ferns found primarily in the Paleogene and Eocene periods. intro: Botryopteris crenata, also known as the narrow-crenate strap fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Osmundaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
